raw_consumer_statement:
I noticed XXXX charges on my XXXX 's JetBlue credit card that were not made by me. 

The first is for XXXX XXXX  on XX/XX/year> for {$1600.00} and the second is for XXXX XXXX on XX/XX/year> for {$140.00}. 

Neither of those charges were made by me or anyone else authorized to use my credit card. Actually, no one other than myself is authorized to use my card. The bank did tell me that the charges were made at stores in the XXXX area. I was in Florida that day. 

I did not make either charge. 

On XX/XX/year>, I was in XXXX  on the last day of a vacation I had. I did make various charges using my card on XX/XX/year> showing that I was in Florida on that date. I made charges on the card at a restaurant, parking meter, car rental company and a gas station, all in XXXX, XXXX  on that date. XXXX 's has all of that information. 

Despite my repeated call to Barclay 's Bank XXXX department and my XXXX written notices to them, all I'm being told by them is that the charges were valid and that I would be responsible for them. I have asked Barclay 's to show me why they believe that the charges were valid, but they have refused to provide me with anything to support their position. I imagine that is because they have nothing, as I did not make the charges. 

I believe the charges must be reversed together with any interest I have been charged.
